svg.text: frame strip: idle(2f) → walk(4f loop) → attack(3f) → hit(2f) → death(4f)
goblin: HP 20 | DMG 4 | SPD fast | detection 3 tiles | color: #5aaa4a
orc: HP 60 | DMG 10 | SPD slow | detection 4 tiles | color: #7a6a3a
skeleton: HP 30 | DMG 8 | SPD medium | detection 5 tiles | range 4 | color: #ddddc8
// sprite sheet layout (atlas mode in godot)
idle[0] walk[1] walk[2] attack[3] hit[4] death[5]
1. Export each character row as PNG strip (192×32)
3. Add AnimatedSprite2D node to character scene
8. Scale 2x–4x in viewport for visible pixel art
hit frame: full white flash in Godot via modulate = Color.WHITE | death: hide after 0.5s delay + particle poof
player: idle×2 walk×4 attack×3 hit×2 death×4 = 15 frames
goblin: idle×2 walk×2 attack×3 hit×2 death×3 = 12 frames
orc: idle×2 walk×2 attack×3 hit×2 death×3 = 12 frames
skeleton: idle×2 walk×2 attack×4 hit×2 death×4 = 14 frames
recommended FPS: walk=8 idle=4 attack=12 hit=10 death=6
all sprites: 32×32px | top-down view | original artwork — 100% free to use commercially
